about.title: About Me
about.headline: Software Engineer specializing in modern web interfaces
about.description: I build production-ready web applications using React, Next.js, and TypeScript. With experience across the full development lifecycle, I deliver clean, maintainable code and pixel-perfect implementations. My background transitioning from military service has instilled strong discipline, problem-solving skills, and the ability to thrive in fast-paced environments.

opsTracker.tagline: Production-grade Jira-like issue tracker — demonstrating scalable Next.js architecture with Supabase, RBAC, and real-world engineering patterns.
keyProps: Server Components + React Query hybrid data model
RBAC enforced at DB level via Supabase RLS
Handles large datasets with TanStack Virtual
opsTracker.description: Ops Tracker is a <highlight>full-stack</highlight> project management and issue tracking application demonstrating <highlight>production-level</highlight> frontend architecture backed by a real <highlight>Supabase</highlight> (PostgreSQL) database. It covers authentication, <highlight>role-based access control</highlight>, <highlight>Kanban boards</highlight>, project-scoped issues, <highlight>full audit trails</highlight>, <highlight>transactional email</highlight>, and <highlight>internationalisation</highlight> across three languages — everything a real <highlight>B2B SaaS</highlight> requires.

highlights.title: Next.js 16 App Router
highlights.description: Server Components, Server Actions, and proxy middleware drive both authentication and i18n end-to-end.

highlights.title: Row Level Security at the DB
highlights.description: Supabase RLS enforces every read and write at the database layer — zero-trust by default.

highlights.title: Three-Tier RBAC
highlights.description: User, Admin, and Super Admin roles are stored as a typed Postgres app_role enum and validated server-side.

highlights.title: Virtualised Issue Table
highlights.description: TanStack Table v8 paired with @tanstack/react-virtual keeps large issue lists smooth at any row count.

highlights.title: Playwright E2E Coverage
highlights.description: Critical authentication and issue workflows are guarded by end-to-end Playwright tests.

architectureSnapshot.title: Feature-based modules
architectureSnapshot.description: Each domain lives under src/features/<domain>/ with its components, hooks, actions, service, and Zod schemas colocated.

architectureSnapshot.title: Server-first data fetching
architectureSnapshot.description: Server Components handle the initial paint; TanStack Query takes over on the client for lists, filters, and optimistic mutations.

architectureSnapshot.title: Defense in depth
architectureSnapshot.description: Supabase RLS plus Server Action role checks enforce permissions twice — the client is never trusted for authorization.

architectureSnapshot.title: Append-only audit log
architectureSnapshot.description: Every mutating action is written to a PostgreSQL audit_logs table with typed action keys, actor, entity, and metadata.

defenseInDepth.label: RLS at the DB + Server Action checks

rlsAtDb.challenge: Client-side authorization is fragile — any leaked token or hand-crafted request can bypass UI guards.
rlsAtDb.solution: Authorization was pushed down into PostgreSQL via Supabase Row Level Security, with Server Actions performing a second role check before any mutation runs.
rlsAtDb.tradeoff: RLS policies are harder to debug than `if` statements and every table needs explicit coverage — but the system is zero-trust by default and the client is never trusted with permissions.

rscVsClient.challenge: Server Components give a fast, SEO-friendly first paint, but lists, filters, and inline editing need real client interactivity.
rscVsClient.solution: A hybrid model: Server Components prefetch data and dehydrate it into a HydrationBoundary, then TanStack Query takes over on the client for filtering, sorting, and optimistic updates.
rscVsClient.tradeoff: Two data layers raise the mental-model overhead — every feature has to decide where its data lives — but first paint stays fast and the UI never feels static.

auditLog.challenge: B2B SaaS needs full traceability: who did what, when, and to which entity — without coupling that bookkeeping to feature code.
auditLog.solution: An append-only PostgreSQL audit_logs table is written from a single, typed audit service. Action keys are a literal union, so every mutation has to declare its intent at the type level.
auditLog.tradeoff: Write amplification and storage growth are real, but the audit trail is the source of truth for compliance, debugging, and the in-app activity timelines.

monorepoFeatures.challenge: A flat src/components + src/hooks layout collapses under its own weight once a project has multiple bounded contexts.
monorepoFeatures.solution: Code is organised by feature (src/features/<domain>/) with components, hooks, server actions, services, and schemas colocated next to the domain they belong to.
monorepoFeatures.tradeoff: Cross-feature reuse takes a deliberate promotion step into shared/, but ownership is obvious and refactoring a single domain rarely touches anything outside its folder.

kanbanBoard.label: Drag-and-Drop Kanban Board
kanbanBoard.description: Per-project Kanban board powered by @dnd-kit with columns driven by configurable workflow statuses.

rbac.label: Role-Based Access Control
rbac.description: Three-tier RBAC — User, Admin, Super Admin — enforced at the database layer via Supabase RLS.

virtualTable.label: Virtualised Issue Table
virtualTable.description: Large issue lists rendered with TanStack Table v8 and @tanstack/react-virtual for smooth performance at scale.

auditTrail.label: Full Audit Trail
auditTrail.description: Every mutating action is recorded to PostgreSQL audit_logs with colour-coded action badges and meaningful summaries.

i18n.label: Internationalisation
i18n.description: Full i18n via next-intl across English, German, and Slovenian including all validation messages and error text.

adminPanel.label: Admin Control Panel
adminPanel.description: Dedicated /admin area for user role management, issue status CRUD, global audit log, and super-admin settings.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.lead: How this site collects, uses, and protects your personal data — written in plain language and aligned with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

collect.title: What we collect
collect.body: Only what you give us — name, email, and message via the contact form. Plus minimal technical data for security.

share.title: Who it goes to
share.body: A small set of trusted processors: Resend for email, Vercel for hosting, and Google reCAPTCHA for spam protection.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: Access, correct, delete, port, or restrict your data — and withdraw consent or complain to a supervisory authority at any time.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.body: This Privacy Policy explains how personal data is processed when you visit renekrajnc.com (the "Site"). The Site is a personal portfolio operated by an individual based in the European Union. We respect your privacy and only process the personal data that is strictly necessary to operate the Site, respond to your enquiries, and keep the Site secure. By using the Site you acknowledge that you have read this Policy. If you do not agree with it, please do not use the Site.

controller.title: Who is responsible (Data Controller)
controller.body: The data controller responsible for your personal data under the GDPR is Rene Krajnc, based in Maribor, Slovenia. For any privacy-related question, request, or complaint you can contact us at info@renekrajnc.com. We will respond to verified requests without undue delay and at the latest within 30 days, in line with Article 12(3) GDPR.

data-we-collect.title: Personal data we collect
data-we-collect.body: We collect only the data needed to operate the Site and respond to you. Specifically:
bullets: Contact form data: the name, email address, and message you submit through the contact form.
Communication content: any further correspondence you send to us by email.
Technical data: IP address, user agent, referrer, and timestamps recorded in server access and security logs by our hosting provider.
Anti-abuse signals: a reCAPTCHA token and the score Google returns, used to block automated form submissions.
Preference data: your selected theme (light or dark), stored locally in your browser; this never leaves your device.

purposes-and-legal-bases.title: Purposes and legal bases
purposes-and-legal-bases.body: We process your personal data only for clearly defined purposes and only where we have a valid legal basis under Article 6(1) GDPR:
bullets: To answer your enquiry sent via the contact form — legal basis: pre-contractual or contractual measures and our legitimate interest in responding to messages (Art. 6(1)(b) and (f) GDPR).
To protect the contact form against spam and abuse using Google reCAPTCHA — legal basis: your prior consent given via the cookie banner (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR).
To keep the Site available and secure (server logs, rate-limiting, abuse detection) — legal basis: our legitimate interest in operating a secure service (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR).

To remember your theme preference — this is stored locally and is not a legal-basis question under GDPR because no personal data is processed by us, but it relies on a strictly necessary technical mechanism.
To comply with legal obligations such as responding to verified data subject requests or lawful authority requests — legal basis: legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR).

cookies.title: Cookies and similar technologies
cookies.body: The Site uses a minimal number of cookies and local storage entries. We do not use advertising cookies, cross-site tracking, or behavioural profiling.
bullets: Strictly necessary: a 'theme' value stored in your browser's localStorage so the Site remembers your light/dark choice. No consent is required for this because it is strictly necessary to deliver the service you requested.

Functional / security (consent-based): when you accept cookies, we load Google reCAPTCHA v3 on the contact page. reCAPTCHA may set cookies on Google domains and collect technical data to distinguish humans from bots.

Consent management: a small 'cookie-consent-v1' entry is stored in your browser's localStorage to remember your choice between Accept and Reject. You can change your choice at any time via the 'Cookie preferences' link in the footer.

third-parties.title: Service providers and recipients
third-parties.body: We never sell personal data. We only share it with a small set of trusted service providers acting as data processors on our behalf, each bound by an appropriate data processing agreement:
bullets: Resend (Resend, Inc., USA) — transactional email delivery for contact form messages. See <resendLink>Resend's privacy policy</resendLink>.
Google reCAPTCHA (Google Ireland Ltd / Google LLC, USA) — bot and abuse protection on the contact form, only loaded after you accept cookies. See <googleLink>Google's privacy policy</googleLink>.
Vercel (Vercel Inc., USA) — hosting, content delivery, and server logging. See <vercelLink>Vercel's privacy policy</vercelLink>.

international-transfers.title: International data transfers
international-transfers.body: Some of the processors listed above are based in the United States. Where personal data is transferred outside the European Economic Area, the transfer is protected by appropriate safeguards under Chapter V GDPR — typically the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses and, where applicable, the EU–US Data Privacy Framework. You can request a copy of the safeguards in place by contacting us at <mailLink>info@renekrajnc.com</mailLink>.

retention.title: How long we keep your data
retention.body: We keep personal data only for as long as it is needed for the purpose it was collected for, or as required by law:
bullets: Contact form messages and related email correspondence: up to 12 months after our last exchange, unless an ongoing business relationship or a legal obligation requires longer retention.
Server access and security logs: up to 30 days, after which they are deleted or anonymised by our hosting provider.
reCAPTCHA assessments: retained by Google for the periods described in their privacy policy; we do not store the raw token after verification.
Cookie consent record: stored locally in your browser until you clear it or change your choice.

your-rights.title: Your rights under the GDPR
your-rights.body: Subject to the conditions in the GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
bullets: Right of access (Art. 15) — obtain confirmation of whether we process your data and a copy of it.
Right to rectification (Art. 16) — have inaccurate or incomplete data corrected.
Right to erasure (Art. 17) — request deletion of your data where the legal grounds for processing no longer apply.
Right to restriction (Art. 18) — restrict processing in defined situations, for example while you contest accuracy.
Right to data portability (Art. 20) — receive data you provided to us in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format.
Right to object (Art. 21) — object to processing based on legitimate interests, including direct outreach.
Right to withdraw consent (Art. 7(3)) — withdraw any consent you have given at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of processing before withdrawal.

Right to lodge a complaint — file a complaint with a supervisory authority, in particular the Slovenian Information Commissioner (<dpaLink>Informacijski pooblaščenec</dpaLink>) or the authority in your EU country of residence.

security.title: How we protect your data
security.body: We use industry-standard measures to keep your data safe in transit and at rest:
bullets: All traffic to the Site is encrypted in transit via TLS (HTTPS).
Strict Content Security Policy, HSTS, and other security headers are configured at the framework level.
Secrets such as API keys are stored in environment variables on the hosting platform and never exposed to the browser.
Google reCAPTCHA v3 protects the contact form against automated abuse.
We do not collect payment data, special-category data, or government identifiers through the Site.

children.title: Children's privacy
children.body: The Site is intended for a general professional audience and is not directed at children under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe a child has provided personal data through the Site, please contact us at <mailLink>info@renekrajnc.com</mailLink> and we will delete it without undue delay.

changes.title: Changes to this policy
changes.body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, third-party services, or legal requirements. The current version is always available on this page and the Last updated date at the top of the page is the authoritative version marker. Material changes will be highlighted on the Site before they take effect.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.body: If you have any question about this Privacy Policy, want to exercise a data subject right, or would like to raise a concern, please reach out at <mailLink>info@renekrajnc.com</mailLink> or via the <contactLink>contact page</contactLink>. We aim to respond to all verified requests within 30 days, in line with Article 12(3) GDPR.

seo.title: Privacy Policy | Rene Krajnc
seo.description: Privacy Policy for renekrajnc.com — what personal data is collected, why, who it is shared with, and how your GDPR rights are respected.

banner.title: We value your privacy
banner.body: This site uses strictly necessary technologies to remember your theme preference. With your consent we also load Google reCAPTCHA on the contact form to block spam. Read our <link>Privacy Policy</link> for details.

contactNotice.message: Spam protection is disabled. Accept cookies to enable Google reCAPTCHA and send your message.

workExperience.title: Work Experience
workExperience.subtitle: Delivering production-ready frontend solutions with React, Next.js and TypeScript for 6+ years.

highlights: Build the frontend (React, Next.js, TypeScript) for modern web products
Build scalable web apps with React, Next.js, TypeScript and React Query
Mentor junior developers, conduct code reviews and drive frontend best practices
Contribute to architectural decisions for production-grade, high-traffic web apps
Built React applications for Slovenian Railways, Arriva, Nomago and Marprom
Developed ticket purchasing platforms and public transport passenger apps
Created interfaces for ticket vending machines deployed at train stations across Slovenia
Integrated backend APIs ensuring reliable data flow across complex transport systems
Developed web portals and admin interfaces for a smart parcel delivery SaaS platform
Built user, company and delivery-box management UIs serving an international client base
Integrated APIs and developed reusable UI components within a DotNetNuke CMS
Collaborated in an international agile team delivering cross-border logistics solutions
